data.title: Is It Too Late To Consider National Bank of Canada (TSX:NA) After Strong Multi‑Year Gains?
data.description:
• If you are wondering whether National Bank of Canada at about C$180 is still offering value or if most of the opportunity is already reflected in the price, this article walks through what the current market is implying and how that stacks up against different valuation checks.
  • The stock has had a mixed run recently, with a 0.3% move over the past week, a 5.4% decline over the last 30 days, a 3.9% return year to date, and a much stronger 55.1% return over the past year, along with a 106.9% return over three years and 154.1% over five years.
  • Recent coverage around Canadian banks has focused on the sector's sensitivity to interest rates, capital requirements, and overall credit quality. This helps frame how investors think about banks like National Bank of Canada. These themes give useful context for the share price path you see today, as the market weighs earnings resilience, capital strength, and the outlook for growth.
  • Right now, National Bank of Canada scores 2 out of 6 on Simply Wall St's valuation checks. Next you will see how traditional metrics like P/E and discounted cash flow compare, and then finish with a more complete way to think about what the current price really means for you.

National Bank of Canada scores just 2/6 on our valuation checks. See what other red flags we found in the full valuation breakdown.

Advertisement

Approach 1: National Bank of Canada Excess Returns Analysis

The Excess Returns model looks at how much value a bank can create above the return that equity investors require, based on its profitability and book value per share. Instead of focusing on cash flows, it starts with what the bank earns on its equity and compares that to the cost of equity.

For National Bank of Canada, the model uses a Book Value of CA$81.38 per share and a Stable EPS of CA$14.26 per share, based on weighted future Return on Equity estimates from 9 analysts. The Average Return on Equity used is 16.16%, while the Cost of Equity is CA$6.39 per share. That leaves an Excess Return estimate of CA$7.87 per share, which is then applied to a Stable Book Value of CA$88.23 per share, sourced from weighted future Book Value estimates from 8 analysts.

Putting this together, the Excess Returns model arrives at an intrinsic value of about CA$268.48 per share, which implies the stock is 33.0% undervalued relative to the current price around CA$180.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Excess Returns analysis suggests National Bank of Canada is undervalued by 33.0%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 10 more high quality undervalued stocks.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for National Bank of Canada.

Approach 2: National Bank of Canada Price vs Earnings

P/E is a common way to value profitable banks because it links what you pay directly to the earnings the business is generating today. A higher or lower P/E often reflects what the market is pricing in for future growth and how much risk investors see in those earnings.

National Bank of Canada currently trades on a P/E of 16.92x. That sits above the Banks industry average of 10.79x and also above the peer group average of 13.33x, which suggests the market is willing to pay more for each dollar of earnings compared with many other banks.

Simply Wall St also provides a Fair Ratio of 15.12x for National Bank of Canada. This is a proprietary estimate of what a reasonable P/E could be for this specific company, based on factors such as its earnings growth profile, profit margins, risk characteristics, industry and market cap. Because it is tailored to the company rather than relying only on broad peers or industry averages, it can offer a more focused reference point for you as an investor. With the current P/E above the Fair Ratio, the shares screen as somewhat expensive on this metric.

Result: OVERVALUED

P/E ratios tell one story, but what if the real opportunity lies elsewhere? Start investing in legacies, not executives. Discover our 3 top founder-led companies.

Upgrade Your Decision Making: Choose your National Bank of Canada Narrative

Earlier it was mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation. Narratives on Simply Wall St let you set a clear story for National Bank of Canada, link that story to your own revenue, earnings and margin forecasts, and turn those into a Fair Value you can compare with the current price to decide whether the stock looks attractive or stretched. The platform keeps that view updated as new news or earnings arrive, and different investors can express very different takes. For example, one Narrative might lean closer to the higher analyst target of about CA$205 if you think the bank can fully realize the buyback plans and earnings path. Another might sit nearer the lower CA$153 target if you are more cautious about concentration in Quebec, mortgage risk or long term profitability.

data.title: Dexxos Participações' (BVMF:DEXP3) Solid Earnings May Rest On Weak Foundations
data.description:
Dexxos Participações S.A.'s (BVMF:DEXP3) healthy profit numbers didn't contain any surprises for investors. We think this is due to investors looking beyond the statutory profits and being concerned with what they see.

Trump has pledged to "unleash" American oil and gas and these 15 US stocks have developments that are poised to benefit.

To understand the value of a company's earnings growth, it is imperative to consider any dilution of shareholders' interests. Dexxos Participações expanded the number of shares on issue by 12% over the last year. As a result, its net income is now split between a greater number of shares. Per share metrics like EPS help us understand how much actual shareholders are benefitting from the company's profits, while the net income level gives us a better view of the company's absolute size. You can see a chart of Dexxos Participações' EPS by clicking here.

Advertisement

How Is Dilution Impacting Dexxos Participações' Earnings Per Share (EPS)?

Dexxos Participações' net profit dropped by 4.2% per year over the last three years. However, profit was steady in the last year. Having said that, earnings per share were also flat, but nonetheless grew less than profit. So you can see that the dilution has had a bit of an impact on shareholders.

In the long term, earnings per share growth should beget share price growth. So Dexxos Participações shareholders will want to see that EPS figure continue to increase. However, if its profit increases while its earnings per share stay flat (or even fall) then shareholders might not see much benefit. For the ordinary retail shareholder, EPS is a great measure to check your hypothetical "share" of the company's profit.

That might leave you wondering what analysts are forecasting in terms of future profitability. Luckily, you can click here to see an interactive graph depicting future profitability, based on their estimates.

Our Take On Dexxos Participações' Profit Performance

Dexxos Participações issued shares during the year, and that means its EPS performance lags its net income growth. Therefore, it seems possible to us that Dexxos Participações' true underlying earnings power is actually less than its statutory profit. But at least holders can take some solace from the 6.0% EPS growth in the last year. At the end of the day, it's essential to consider more than just the factors above, if you want to understand the company properly. With this in mind, we wouldn't consider investing in a stock unless we had a thorough understanding of the risks. data.title: Some May Be Optimistic About JSL's (BVMF:JSLG3) Earnings
data.description:
Soft earnings didn't appear to concern JSL S.A.'s (BVMF:JSLG3) shareholders over the last week. Our analysis suggests that while the profits are soft, the foundations of the business are strong.

This technology could replace computers: discover the 20 stocks are working to make quantum computing a reality.

Advertisement

Examining Cashflow Against JSL's Earnings

One key financial ratio used to measure how well a company converts its profit to free cash flow (FCF) is the accrual ratio. In plain english, this ratio subtracts FCF from net profit, and divides that number by the company's average operating assets over that period. The ratio shows us how much a company's profit exceeds its FCF.

As a result, a negative accrual ratio is a positive for the company, and a positive accrual ratio is a negative. That is not intended to imply we should worry about a positive accrual ratio, but it's worth noting where the accrual ratio is rather high. That's because some academic studies have suggested that high accruals ratios tend to lead to lower profit or less profit growth.

JSL has an accrual ratio of -0.14 for the year to December 2025. That indicates that its free cash flow was a fair bit more than its statutory profit. In fact, it had free cash flow of R$1.1b in the last year, which was a lot more than its statutory profit of R$81.7m. Given that JSL had negative free cash flow in the prior corresponding period, the trailing twelve month resul of R$1.1b would seem to be a step in the right direction. Importantly, we note an unusual tax situation, which we discuss below, has impacted the accruals ratio.

That might leave you wondering what analysts are forecasting in terms of future profitability. Luckily, you can click here to see an interactive graph depicting future profitability, based on their estimates.

An Unusual Tax Situation

Moving on from the accrual ratio, we note that JSL profited from a tax benefit which contributed R$84m to profit. This is of course a bit out of the ordinary, given it is more common for companies to be paying tax than receiving tax benefits! The receipt of a tax benefit is obviously a good thing, on its own. However, our data indicates that tax benefits can temporarily boost statutory profit in the year it is booked, but subsequently profit may fall back. In the likely event the tax benefit is not repeated, we'd expect to see its statutory profit levels drop, at least in the absence of strong growth. While we think it's good that the company has booked a tax benefit, it does mean that there's every chance the statutory profit will come in a lot higher than it would be if the income was adjusted for one-off factors.

Our Take On JSL's Profit Performance

In conclusion, JSL has strong cashflow relative to earnings, which indicates good quality earnings, but the tax benefit means its profit wasn't as sustainable as we'd like to see. Considering the aforementioned, we think that JSL's profits are probably a reasonable reflection of its underlying profitability; although we'd be confident in that conclusion if we saw a cleaner set of results. If you want to do dive deeper into JSL, you'd also look into what risks it is currently facing. For instance, we've identified 4 warning signs for JSL (2 make us uncomfortable) you should be familiar with.

In this article we've looked at a number of factors that can impair the utility of profit numbers, as a guide to a business. But there is always more to discover if you are capable of focussing your mind on minutiae. data.title: Why Boa Safra Sementes' (BVMF:SOJA3) Shaky Earnings Are Just The Beginning Of Its Problems
data.description:
Last week's earnings announcement from Boa Safra Sementes S.A. (BVMF:SOJA3) was disappointing to investors, with a sluggish profit figure. We did some further digging and think they have a few more reasons to be concerned beyond the statutory profit.

We've found 21 US stocks that are forecast to pay a dividend yield of over 6% next year. See the full list for free.

Advertisement

An Unusual Tax Situation

Boa Safra Sementes reported a tax benefit of R$28m, which is well worth noting. This is meaningful because companies usually pay tax rather than receive tax benefits. The receipt of a tax benefit is obviously a good thing, on its own. However, the devil in the detail is that these kind of benefits only impact in the year they are booked, and are often one-off in nature. In the likely event the tax benefit is not repeated, we'd expect to see its statutory profit levels drop, at least in the absence of strong growth.

That might leave you wondering what analysts are forecasting in terms of future profitability. Luckily, you can click here to see an interactive graph depicting future profitability, based on their estimates.

Our Take On Boa Safra Sementes' Profit Performance

Boa Safra Sementes reported that it received a tax benefit, rather than paid tax, in its last report. As a result we don't think its profit result, which includes that tax-boost, is a good guide to its sustainable profit levels. Therefore, it seems possible to us that Boa Safra Sementes' true underlying earnings power is actually less than its statutory profit. Sadly, its EPS was down over the last twelve months. The goal of this article has been to assess how well we can rely on the statutory earnings to reflect the company's potential, but there is plenty more to consider. In light of this, if you'd like to do more analysis on the company, it's vital to be informed of the risks involved. Case in point: We've spotted 2 warning signs for Boa Safra Sementes you should be aware of.

data.title: Assessing Partners Value Investments (TSXV:PVF.UN) Valuation After Steady EPS And Softer Annual Earnings Results
data.description:
Partners Value Investments (TSXV:PVF.UN) is in focus after releasing its full year 2025 results, reporting revenue of US$133.27 million, net income of US$69.08 million and largely unchanged earnings per share.

See our latest analysis for Partners Value Investments.

The results landed alongside a sharp 7.38% 1 day share price return to CA$17.18. However, the 90 day share price return of 4.56% and year to date return of 1.83% suggest momentum has cooled, even as 1 year and 5 year total shareholder returns of 14.54% and 201.40% keep the longer term picture positive.

If this kind of performance has you thinking about what else might be worth a closer look, it could be a good time to broaden your search with 3 top founder-led companies

With revenue at US$133.27 million, net income at US$69.08 million and EPS largely flat, plus a mixed recent share price record, the key question is simple: is there still a buying opportunity here, or is the market already pricing in future growth?

Advertisement

Preferred P/E of 146.5x: Is it justified?

Partners Value Investments is trading on a P/E of 146.5x, which is far higher than both its industry peers and the broader Canadian Capital Markets average at the last close of CA$17.18.

The P/E ratio compares the current share price to earnings per share, so a higher P/E usually means investors are paying more today for each unit of current earnings. For an investment holding company like Partners Value Investments, a rich P/E can sometimes reflect expectations around underlying investment gains or future profitability rather than just recent reported earnings.

Here though, the story is more cautious. Earnings have declined by 0.6% per year over the past 5 years, net profit margins have slipped from 57.2% to 44.6%, and there has been a large one off gain of US$20.1m affecting the latest 12 month numbers. That mix suggests the current earnings base is both lower quality and under pressure, which makes a very high P/E harder to align with recent performance.

Compared to the Canadian Capital Markets industry average P/E of 9.2x and a peer average P/E that is actually negative, Partners Value Investments stands out as expensive on earnings. The market is putting a much richer price tag on each dollar of earnings here than it is on the sector overall, which is a clear signal that the valuation is stretched against these benchmarks.

DeFi Development (DFDV) just wrapped up FY 2025 with Q4 total revenue of US$4.5 million and a basic EPS loss of US$5.48, while trailing twelve month revenue sat at US$11.4 million against a basic EPS loss of US$4.00. Over the past six reported quarters, the company has seen quarterly revenue move from US$0.62 million in Q4 2024 to US$4.5 million in Q4 2025, with EPS swinging between a loss of US$0.05 and a profit of US$2.41 before landing back in loss-making territory. This leaves investors focused on how much of the top line is translating into sustainable margins.

See our full analysis for DeFi Development.

With the headline results set, the next step is to see how these revenue and EPS swings line up against the widely held narratives around DeFi Development's growth potential and earnings risk.

Curious how numbers become stories that shape markets? Explore Community Narratives

Advertisement

Revenue Jumps To US$11.4 Million Over Twelve Months

  • On a trailing twelve month basis, total revenue reached US$11.4 million, compared with quarterly revenue of US$4.5 million in Q4 2025 and US$0.63 million in Q4 2024. This means the business is now operating off a much larger top line than a year ago.
  • What stands out for a bullish take is that this higher revenue base sits beside forecasts for about 23.8% yearly revenue growth, yet trailing twelve month net income is still a loss of US$73.8 million. This means:
    • Supporters who focus on growth can point to revenue moving from under US$2.1 million in trailing revenue at Q4 2024 to US$11.4 million at Q4 2025 as evidence the commercial model is gaining scale.
    • At the same time, the size of the trailing loss and the Q4 2025 net loss of US$144.5 million remind readers that, even with growth, the income statement is far from breakeven. As a result, the bullish view is heavily tied to how that revenue is used rather than current profitability.

Curious how these numbers are shaping different storylines around DeFi Development, and where others think the growth could lead? 📊 Read the what the Community is saying about DeFi Development.

Swings From US$56.0 Million Profit To US$144.5 Million Loss

  • Quarterly net income moved from a profit of US$56.0 million in Q3 2025 to a loss of US$144.5 million in Q4 2025, while trailing twelve month EPS fell from a profit of US$4.90 at Q3 2025 to a loss of US$4.00 at Q4 2025. This underscores how sensitive earnings are to short term changes.
  • Critics who lean bearish will point to these swings as evidence of fragile profitability, and the five year pattern of rising losses reinforces that concern, because:
    • The company is described as unprofitable on trailing results, with losses having increased by about 0.8% per year over five years. This fits with the sharp move from a quarterly EPS profit of US$2.41 in Q3 2025 to a loss of US$5.48 in Q4 2025.
    • This pattern can make it harder for a cautious reader to rely on any single profitable quarter, since the trailing twelve month loss of US$73.8 million shows that short bursts of profit have not yet translated into consistent positive net margin.

P/S Of 8.6x With Less Than One Year Cash Runway

  • The shares trade on a P/S of 8.6x compared with 0.3x for peers and 1.2x for the US Consumer Finance industry. This premium sits alongside a business that is loss making on a trailing twelve month basis and has less than one year of cash runway flagged as a risk.
  • What is challenging for a bullish holder at the current share price of US$3.29 is that this rich P/S multiple is paired with several balance sheet and capital structure flags, so:
    • Recent substantial shareholder dilution means any future value creation needs to work harder on a per share basis. This matters when the company is not yet reporting positive trailing earnings.
    • High share price volatility relative to the US market and the short cash runway both add pressure for the business to show that the forecast 23.8% revenue growth can eventually support the current valuation multiples.

Next Steps

Don't just look at this quarter; the real story is in the long-term trend. We've done an in-depth analysis on DeFi Development's growth and its valuation to see if today's price is a bargain. Add the company to your watchlist or portfolio now so you don't miss the next big move.

With all of this in mind, are you leaning more cautious or optimistic about DeFi Development, and how quickly do you want to firm up your stance? To balance the upside stories with the concerns that keep coming up in the data, take a closer look at the 1 key reward and 4 important warning signs

To own Zeta Global, you need to believe its AI-first marketing cloud can keep turning vast data into clearly measurable outcomes for large enterprises, while eventually improving profitability. Athena by Zeta looks directionally supportive of that story by promising faster, ROI-linked decisioning, but it does not by itself resolve Zeta’s biggest near term concern: the tension between ongoing AI investment and the company’s still uneven path to consistent GAAP profitability.

The launch of Athena builds directly on Zeta’s earlier AI Agent Studio and agentic workflows, which already aimed to automate complex, multi step marketing tasks across channels. Together, these products tighten the link between prediction, activation, and revenue accountability inside the Zeta Marketing Platform, which could matter for key catalysts such as deeper agency adoption and higher revenue per customer if enterprises see Athena as a material upgrade rather than just another AI feature.
